name: character-count
title: Character count
description: Wraps an Input or Textarea with a live remaining-character count. The limit is soft by default (no native maxlength) so users are never cut off mid-word; the count guides while server-side validation decides. Announcements are debounced and the over-limit state sets aria-invalid, never color alone.
useWhen: Giving live feedback on how much text is left in a bounded free-text field
Signalling a soft limit that guides without blocking typing (server validates)
Showing an over-limit warning that also marks the control invalid
avoidWhen: A hard technical limit the user must not exceed — set the native maxlength (or enforce) and explain the cap in the field hint instead
Fixed-length inputs like a ZIP or card number (use input constraints, not a count)
Content with no meaningful length limit

keyboard: Adds no keyboard behavior of its own; the wrapped control keeps its native contract
The wrapped control stays a single tab stop
accessibility.targetSize: not applicable (the wrapper is non-interactive; the wrapped control meets the 44px project default)
accessibility.notes: The visible count is not itself a live region, so it never announces per keystroke. A separate visually-hidden aria-live="polite" region re-announces the count only after the user pauses (announceDelay, default 1000ms). The wrapped control references the visible count via aria-describedby. Over the limit the control gets aria-invalid so the error is signalled by the control's error ring, not color alone (WCAG 1.4.1).
